about: First constructed by the Urartians in the 8th century BC upon steep and intimidating rocky cliffs, Harput Castle became one of the hardest fortresses to conquer in Anatolia with its unique vantage point overlooking the Elazığ plain. Dominating the caravan routes descending into Mesopotamia, this massive structure was frequented over centuries by countless civilizations including the Romans, Byzantines, Arabs, Seljuks, Artuqids, and Ottomans.
